description: Add a lightweight development-only validation utility that verifies workspace data integrity: all objects have valid workspaceId, all edge source/target references exist, and no object references a non-existent workspace.
steps: STEP 1 - Create src/workspaceValidator.js with a validateWorkspaces function that checks: (a) every node, group, pin, image, and edge has a valid workspaceId, (b) every edge source and target reference an existing object (node, group, or image), (c) no object references a workspace that does not exist.
STEP 2 - The utility should output warnings/errors via console.warn and console.error.
STEP 3 - Import and call the validator in App.jsx at key points (after imports, after migrations, after exports) gated behind import.meta.env.DEV.
STEP 4 - Run npm run build to verify no compilation errors.
acceptance_criteria: src/workspaceValidator.js exists with a validateWorkspaces function
The validator checks that every node, group, pin, image, and edge has a valid workspaceId
The validator checks that every edge source and target reference an existing object
The validator checks that no object references a workspace that does not exist
The validator uses console.warn/console.error for output
The validator is gated behind import.meta.env.DEV (only runs in dev builds)
npm run build completes successfully with no errors
verification: Run 'npm run build' in /projects/sandbox/Clear-mind and confirm it exits with code 0 and no errors
findings: Created src/workspaceValidator.js with validateWorkspaces() function. Integrated at 7 call sites in App.jsx: init migration, project switch, project delete/switch, project open, handleImport, importAllData, and exportData. All gated behind import.meta.env.DEV. Build passes cleanly.
